title: Cured Salty Chinese Pork Stir-fry
description: A quick, dense pork stir-fry that balances salty umami with a sweet twist from a melted Mars bar, all while staying FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: Pat the pork belly dry and rub with sea salt, letting it cure for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Heat a wok over high heat and add sesame oil.
Add the pork belly, searing all sides until golden brown, about 3–4 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium, then toss in minced ginger and red chili flakes, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour tamari, rice vinegar, and water into the wok, scraping up any browned bits.
Add the melted Mars bar, stirring to create a glossy, sweet-salty sauce.
Simmer the pork until it absorbs the sauce and reaches an internal temperature of 71 °C (160 °F), about 5–6 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning with a pinch more tamari or salt if needed.
Remove from heat, garnish with toasted sesame seeds, and serve hot.
